methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Axelera AI is a Series B-stage semiconductor design company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor comparables, spanning AI chip architecture and specialized silicon vendors at similar or adjacent funding stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $200M raised proxy, implying a $380M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. The peer set includes Etched at $5.0B (Series B), d-Matrix at $2.0B (Series C), Positron AI at $1.0B (Series B), and DEEPX at $529M (Series C). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is not applicable; the semiconductor sector median of 20.3x P/S across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD cannot be deployed without Axelera's disclosed revenue.

The triangulated range across the two applicable methods is $323M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $690M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-adjusted growth rates and the heterogeneity of peer scaling outcomes in frontier semiconductor hardware. Key variables include near-term go-to-market traction and the competitive moat of the core architecture relative to established players.
